channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah Expects Lebanon Inclusion in Any US-Iran Deal
item.description: A leading Hezbollah politician expressed confidence that Iran will insist on Lebanon being included in any ceasefire agreement with the United States, as hopes grow for a diplomatic resolution to the Gulf conflict.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE Stock Markets Hit Two-Month High on Iran Deal Optimism
item.description: Stock markets in the United Arab Emirates surged Friday as regional hopes grew for a potential ceasefire deal between Iran and the United States that could end months of conflict in the Gulf.

item.title: 🚀 SpaceX Makes Historic Stock Market Debut After Record IPO
item.description: SpaceX began trading on Nasdaq after the largest initial public offering in history, with shares opening at $150 and Elon Musk becoming the worlds first trillionaire as investors backed his vision spanning rockets and orbital AI.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Air Force Warns of High-Probability Russian Missile Launch
item.description: Ukraine's Air Force warned of a high probability of a Russian missile launch from the Oreshnik site within the next day, expected to come from the Kapustin Yar launch site in southern Russia.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China-Russia Border River Tests Beijing's Ties With North Korea
item.description: A tiny river on Russia's border is testing China's relationship with Kim Jong-un, as Beijing seeks Sea of Japan access that neither China nor Pyongyang have publicly acknowledged.

item.title: 🤖 Ukraine's Defense AI Chief Predicts 'New Paradigm' of Warfare
item.description: Ukraine's defense AI chief says the system that possesses more data and better understands it will gain advantage, predicting a fundamental shift in how warfare is conducted.

item.title: 🇮🇶 Iran Agrees to Performance-Based Deal Requiring Tehran Concessions
item.description: A White House official said Iran has agreed to a performance-based deal that requires concessions from Tehran, with no frozen assets released until Iran fulfills its commitments.

item.title: 🇮🇶 Iraq Foils Plot to Assassinate National Security Service Chief
item.description: Iraqi security forces foiled a plot to assassinate the National Security Service chief and senior officers, arresting suspects linked to a banned Baath Party front group.

item.title: 🇮🇩 Indonesia's \$8M Visa Scandal Threatens Talent Investment Drive
item.description: An \$8 million visa scandal is threatening Indonesia's efforts to attract foreign talent and investors as the country faces economic strain.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Taps Wall Street's Jay Clayton as Spy Chief
item.description: President Trump appointed former SEC chair Jay Clayton as spy chief following backlash over his previous nominee, choosing a Wall Street veteran to lead the intelligence community.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan Unveils \$452B Annual Budget as Economy Hits Record
item.description: Pakistan unveiled its annual budget as the economy reached a record \$452 billion, marking a significant milestone for the country's economic recovery.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump Says Iran's Leaked Deal Terms Are 'Fake News'
item.description: President Trump denied that the United States made major concessions to Iran, calling leaked reports about the deal terms inaccurate as tensions persist over the proposed agreement.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Extends Losses After Trump Calls Off Iran Strikes
item.description: Oil prices continued to fall as Trump called off planned strikes on Iran, with markets reacting to reduced tensions in the Gulf and the prospect of a diplomatic resolution.

item.title: 🇺🇦 US Lawmakers Seek \$750M for Ukraine in Defense Bill
item.description: US lawmakers proposed \$750 million for Ukraine and sought to rename the Pentagon's department to emphasize war readiness in a new defense bill.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Deal Limbo Leaves World Between Peace and War
item.description: Since announcing a nominal cease-fire two months ago, Iran, Israel and the US have remained locked in low-intensity conflict, with neither side willing to fully commit to peace or escalation.

item.title: 🇪🇺 How EU Can Respond to 'Second China Shock'
item.description: European policymakers are grappling with how to respond to renewed Chinese competition and trade pressures, as the bloc faces economic challenges from Beijing's industrial policies.

item.title: 🇨🇭 Swiss Population Cap Proposal Could Reshape Europe Relations
item.description: Switzerland will vote Sunday on a proposal to cap the population at 10 million in a referendum some have likened to Britain's Brexit vote, with far-reaching consequences for the economy and EU relations.

item.title: 🚀 SpaceX Set for Stock Market Debut After Record IPO
item.description: SpaceX is set for its stock market debut after the world's largest IPO, with Elon Musk becoming the world's first trillionaire as investors bet on his space and AI empire.

item.title: 🇮🇩 Indonesian Students Protest Government Policies Amid Economic Strain
item.description: Indonesian students protested government policies as the country faces economic difficulties, with demonstrations reflecting growing public discontent.

item.title: 🇺🇸 NATO-US Defense Cuts Loom Over Europe Operations
item.description: The US is considering major cuts to fighter jets and warships supporting NATO operations in Europe, raising concerns about the alliance's defense capabilities.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump Says Iran War Deal Close Despite Hormuz Tensions
item.description: President Trump indicated a deal with Iran was close even as tensions over the Strait of Hormuz persist, with conflicting statements from both sides about the terms of an agreement.

item.title: ✡️ Israeli-Palestinian Civil Society Meet in France as Two-State Solution Dims
item.description: Israeli and Palestinian civil society groups met in France to urge the international community not to abandon the two-state solution, as Paris seeks to keep the issue alive amid the broader Middle East conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine War: US Plans Major Cuts to Fighter Jets, Warships for NATO Europe
item.description: The US is planning major cuts to fighter jets and warships supporting NATO operations in Europe, the New York Times reported, raising questions about American commitment to European defense.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran-Backed Militia Threatens Israel-Lebanon Border Region
item.description: Hezbollah expressed confidence that Iran will insist on Lebanon being included in any ceasefire agreement with the United States, as regional powers navigate the complex negotiations.

item.title: ⛽ Oil Prices Fall on Prospects of US-Iran Peace Agreement
item.description: Oil prices extended losses as markets reacted to the prospect of a peace agreement between the US and Iran, reducing geopolitical risk premium in energy markets.
